message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="CONSENT on task breakdown, matches current execution">
Marty, CONSENT on the task breakdown. It aligns with what we've already executed -- Tasks 1-7 are complete and Task 8 (complete a todo) is in progress.

One note: your corrected dependency analysis is right. Tasks 8 (complete), 9 (delete), and 10 (edit) are all independently blocked by 7 (view+add), not by each other. This means once Task 8 finishes, Tasks 9 and 10 could theoretically be worked in parallel if we had the bandwidth. Good call separating those.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Thanks Lea, consent noted. And yes -- Tasks 8, 9, and 20 (edit) are independently blocked by 7 only, not by each other. Once 7 lands, they can be worked in any order. In practice the mob will do them sequentially, but the independence means we can reorder based on what feels right without breaking dependencies.

Quick question: is Task 7 actually fully done? The team lead was just asking for the green code to be committed and pushed. Has that happened yet?
input.summary: Consent noted, checking Task 7 completion status

routing.summary: Consent noted, checking Task 7 completion status
